card.oneLiner: Get your affairs in order, privately. Everything your family would need to find, in one place they can actually find it.
bullets: Full inventory: accounts, property, documents, people
Printable “What My Family Needs to Know” letter

hero.headline: Your family shouldn't have to go looking.
hero.lede: A private, offline organizer that walks you through getting your affairs in order — 44 guided tasks, a full inventory of what you own and owe, and a printable letter that tells your family exactly where everything is. Nothing you type ever leaves your device.
hero.note: Instant download · No subscription · No account · Works on Mac, Windows, tablets & phones
hero.imageAlt: Estate Planning Organizer inventory screen showing accounts, property, and running totals

intro.heading: Everything your family would need, in one place
intro.body: Getting your affairs in order is a vague, heavy to-do that never makes it to the top of the list. The Organizer turns it into a guided path — and done is a gift to your family.

features.title: 44 guided tasks
features.body: Five phases, from taking stock through keeping the plan alive. Every task explains why it matters in plain English — and flags the questions that belong with an attorney.

features.title: Account & asset inventory
features.body: Accounts, property, vehicles, valuables — with institutions, locations, and running totals. Plus a debts register, so the picture your family inherits is complete.

features.title: The document locker
features.body: The 21 documents most families need — will, powers of attorney, deeds, policies — each marked have-it or need-it, with exactly where it lives. A signed document no one can find helps no one.

features.title: Key people & beneficiaries
features.body: Executor, agents under your powers of attorney, guardians, beneficiaries, and the professionals your family will need to call — one list, always current.

features.title: Wishes & instructions
features.body: In your own words: funeral preferences, a healthcare wishes summary, personal messages, pet care, where your digital-account instructions live. The things a form can't carry.

features.title: The Letter of Instruction
features.body: One click compiles it all into a printable “What My Family Needs to Know” packet. Print it, store it with your documents, and re-print whenever life changes.

screens.alt: Guided task list with plain-English explanations
screens.caption: Every step says why it matters.
What you have, what you need, where it lives.
They print into the family letter.

privacy.heading: Private by design, not by promise
privacy.body: You'll be recording account numbers and the location of every important document you own. That information should never touch the internet. In this app, it can't.

cards.title: Zero network requests
cards.body: The app is a single file that runs entirely in your browser, on your device. It makes no connections, calls no servers, and loads nothing from the internet. You can run it with Wi-Fi off.

cards.title: Never a password
cards.body: By design, the Organizer never asks for or stores passwords or PINs. It records that accounts exist and where your written instructions live — the map, never the keys.

cards.title: Yours forever
cards.body: One-time purchase. The file is yours, works offline for life, and the plan inside it stays current with a built-in annual review reminder.

priceCard.sub: Done is a gift to your family.
bullets: Full asset, debt, people & document inventory
Wishes sections & printable Letter of Instruction

faq.a: No. You pay $37 once and the app is yours forever. There is no account, no renewal, and nothing that expires.

No. The Organizer is an organizational tool, not legal documents or legal advice. It gets everything inventoried, located, and written down — and frames the right questions to bring to an attorney, which is how you keep that meeting short.
Never, by design. It records that accounts exist and where your written instructions live. The map, never the keys.

faq.q: Can I buy this for my parents?
faq.a: Yes — many people do. It works on their computer or tablet, needs no account, and working through it together is often the easiest way to start the conversation.
Yes, structurally. The app makes zero network requests — you can verify this with Wi-Fi turned off. Your entries live only on your device, and your backup is a file you control.

faq.q: What if it doesn't work for me?
faq.a: Email hello@quietkeep.co and we'll make it right. We answer every message.

crossSell.heading: Settling an estate instead?
crossSell.body: The Estate Settlement Companion is this organizer's counterpart for executors and families after a death — 47 guided tasks, registers, and the attorney summary.
